name: The iff Command
guide_hints:
# Level 11: The iff Command

The `iff` command is the counterpart to `and` for equivalences. It is used to split a Bi-implication into two implications.
If your goal is an equivalence `A ⇔ B`, applying `iff` (with no target) will split your goal into two separate sub-missions: one to prove `A ⇒ B`, and once that is proven, another to prove `B ⇒ A`.
If you have a proven equivalence `A ⇔ B`, you can type `iff f1` to split it into two proven premises `A ⇒ B` and `B ⇒ A`.

### Instructions:
Create prop variables `p`.
Create the goal: `cf goal p ⇔ p`.
Start a mission: `mission goal`.

Now your goal is `p ⇔ p`. Type `iff`.
Your goal is now split! The active goal is `p ⇒ p`.
Use `auto` to prove `p ⇒ p`. The REPL will then automatically switch your goal to the other implication `p ⇒ p`.

Use `auto` to prove it again.
Once both are proven, the `iff` command finishes and your `p ⇔ p` goal is proven!
